name: next-framework-expert
description: Comprehensive Next.js framework expert covering App Router, Server Components, Server Actions, routing, data fetching, rendering strategies, middleware, and deployment

sources.version_info: Next.js 16 (App Router recommended), Pages Router (legacy support)

can_do: Implement Server Components and Client Components
Use Server Actions for server-side code execution
Implement middleware for authentication and routing
Configure different rendering strategies (SSG, SSR, ISR, CSR)
Use Next.js Image, Font, and Script optimizations
Set up nested layouts and route groups
Implement dynamic routes and catch-all routes
Deploy Next.js applications to Vercel and other platforms
cannot_do: Make architectural decisions about framework selection (defer to architecture-expert)
Choose between Next.js and other React frameworks without business context (defer to architecture-expert)
Configure deployment infrastructure details (defer to infrastructure-expert)

create_next_app.statement: Create Next.js app with: npx create-next-app@latest. Interactive CLI prompts for TypeScript, ESLint, Tailwind CSS, src/ directory, App Router, and Turbopack. App Router is default in Next.js 13+. Creates new Next.js application with optimal defaults.

manual_installation.statement: Manual installation: npm install next react react-dom. Add scripts to package.json: 'dev': 'next dev', 'build': 'next build', 'start': 'next start', 'lint': 'next lint'. Requires Node.js 18.17 or later. Create app/ directory for App Router or pages/ for Pages Router.
manual_installation.example: npm install next react react-dom && npm run dev

overview.statement: App Router (Next.js 13+) is default routing system. File-system based routing in app/ directory. React Server Components by default. Supports layouts, loading states, error boundaries, streaming, and Suspense. Recommended for new projects. Pages Router still supported but App Router preferred.

file_structure.statement: App Router file structure: app/page.js (route), app/layout.js (layout), app/loading.js (loading state), app/error.js (error boundary), app/not-found.js (404 page), app/globals.css (global styles). Special files: layout.js wraps all routes, page.js defines route, loading.js shows while data loads, error.js catches errors.
file_structure.example: app/layout.js wraps app/page.js and app/about/page.js with shared UI

server_components_default.statement: In App Router, components are Server Components by default. Run on server, no client JavaScript sent. Access backend directly (databases, file systems). Use 'use client' directive at top of file to create Client Component. Server Components can import Client Components but not vice versa.
server_components_default.example:
// Server Component (default). export default function Page() { return
Server Component
; } // Client Component. 'use client'; export default function Component() { const [state, setState] = useState(0); }

dynamic_routes.statement: Dynamic routes use brackets: app/blog/[slug]/page.js → /blog/:slug. Access params with props: export default function Page({ params }) { const { slug } = params; }. Generate static params with generateStaticParams() for SSG. Multiple dynamic segments: [id]/[slug]/page.js → /:id/:slug

catch_all_routes.statement: Catch-all routes use [...slug]: app/shop/[...slug]/page.js matches /shop/* (multiple segments). Optional catch-all [[...slug]] matches /shop and /shop/*. Access with params.slug array. Use for complex routing patterns or CMS integration.

route_groups.statement: Route groups organize routes without affecting URL: (marketing)/about/page.js → /about (not /marketing/about). Use parentheses: (marketing), (shop), (admin). Useful for organizing layouts, loading states per section. Route groups don't create URL segments.

parallel_routes.statement: Parallel routes render multiple pages simultaneously: app/@analytics/page.js and app/@team/page.js render together at /dashboard. Use @ prefix for parallel slots. Combine with catch-all for conditional rendering. Useful for dashboards with multiple panels.

intercepting_routes.statement: Intercepting routes intercept route segments: (.) intercepts same level, (..) intercepts parent level, (...) intercepts root. Useful for modals and conditional layouts. Example: app/@modal/(.)photo/[id]/page.js intercepts app/photo/[id]/page.js to show in modal.
intercepting_routes.example: app/@modal/(.)photo/[id]/page.js shows photo in modal instead of full page

nested_layouts.statement: Layouts create shared UI that persists: app/layout.js (root layout, wraps entire app), app/dashboard/layout.js (dashboard layout, wraps dashboard routes). Layouts receive children prop. Can be nested. Layouts don't re-render on navigation, only children do.

layout_structure.statement: Layout file structure: export default function Layout({ children }) { return (<html><body>{children}</body></html>); }. Root layout must include <html> and <body>. Layouts can have metadata, loading states. Layout components are Server Components by default.
layout_structure.example: app/layout.js: export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (<html lang="en"><body>{children}</body></html>); }

server_components_async.statement:
Server Components can be async and fetch data directly: export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('...'); return
{data.title}
; }. No useEffect needed. Fetch requests are automatically deduplicated and cached. Supports native fetch API with Next.js extensions.
server_components_async.example:
export default async function Page() { const res = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); const data = await res.json(); return
{data.title}
; }

fetch_caching.statement: Next.js extends fetch with caching: fetch(url, { cache: 'force-cache' }) for SSG (default), fetch(url, { cache: 'no-store' }) for SSR, fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 60 } }) for ISR (revalidate every 60 seconds). Automatic request deduplication. fetch is async in Server Components.
fetch_caching.example: const data = await fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 3600 } }); // ISR: revalidate every hour

client_data_fetching.statement:
Client Components use useEffect or data fetching libraries (SWR, React Query): 'use client'; import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; export default function Component() { const [data, setData] = useState(null); useEffect(() => { fetch('/api/data').then(r => r.json()).then(setData); }, []); return
{data?.title}
; }
client_data_fetching.example: Client Component with useEffect for client-side data fetching

overview.statement: Server Actions allow calling server functions directly from Client Components. Mark function with 'use server' directive. Can be async. Automatically handle request/response serialization. Support form actions and progressive enhancement. Revalidate cached data automatically.
overview.example: 'use server'; export async function createPost(formData) { // Server code }

form_actions.statement: Server Actions work with forms: <form action={createPost}>. Form data automatically passed to Server Action. Progressive enhancement: works without JavaScript. Use useFormState and useFormStatus hooks for form state management. Server Actions can revalidate data after mutation.

client_call.statement: Call Server Actions from Client Components: import { createPost } from './actions'; 'use client'; export default function Component() { async function handleSubmit() { await createPost(formData); } }. Server Actions are async functions. Can pass serializable data (JSON-serializable).
client_call.example: const result = await createPost({ title: 'Post Title' });

api_endpoints.statement: Route Handlers create API endpoints in App Router: butterfliesapp/api/users/route.js exports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc. Export named functions: export async function GET(request) { return Response.json({ data: [] }); }. Use Request and Response APIs (Web API standard). Support streaming and cookies.
api_endpoints.example: app/api/users/route.js: export async function GET() { return Response.json({ users: [] }); } export async function POST(request) { const body = await request.json(); return Response.json({ created: true }); }

dynamic_route_handlers.statement: Dynamic route handlers: app/api/users/[id]/route.js handles /api/users/:id. Access params: export async function GET(request, { params }) { const { id } = params; }. Same dynamic routing rules as pages: [id], [...slug], [[...slug]].
dynamic_route_handlers.example: app/api/users/[id]/route.js: export async function GET(request, { params }) { const { id } = params.id; }

overview.statement: Middleware runs before request completes. File: middleware.js at project root or src/middleware.js. Export function: export function middleware(request) { const response = NextResponse.next(); return response; }. Runs on edge runtime. Can modify request/response, redirect, rewrite, set headers.
overview.example: middleware.js: export function middleware(request) { if (request.nextUrl.pathname === '/admin') { return NextResponse.redirect('/login'); } }

matcher.statement: Configure middleware matcher to run on specific routes: export const config = { matcher: '/about/:path*' }. Use matcher to limit middleware execution. Matcher patterns: '/about/:path*' matches /about and nested routes, '/api/:path*' matches API routes.

authentication.statement: Middleware commonly used for authentication: check auth token, redirect to login, set headers. Access cookies: request.cookies.get('token'). Create response: NextResponse.next(), NextResponse.redirect(url), NextResponse.rewrite(url). Can read/write headers.

static_generation.statement: Static Site Generation (SSG): Pages generated at build time. Default for pages without dynamic data or with generateStaticParams(). Fastest, cached on CDN. Use for content that doesn't change frequently. In App Router, Server Components with static data are SSG by default.
static_generation.example: Static page generated at build time, served from CDN

server_side_rendering.statement: Server-Side Rendering (SSR): Page generated on each request. Use fetch(url, { cache: 'no-store' }) or dynamic = 'force-dynamic' in App Router. Good for personalized content, real-time data. Slower than SSG but always fresh data.
server_side_rendering.example: export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'; // Force SSR for this route

incremental_static_regeneration.statement: Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR): Statically generate pages, revalidate periodically. Use fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 3600 } }) for ISR. Pages regenerated in background after revalidation period. Best of both worlds: fast static + fresh data. App Router supports ISR by default with revalidate option.
incremental_static_regeneration.example: const data = await fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 60 } }); // Revalidate every 60 seconds

client_side_rendering.statement: Client-Side Rendering (CSR): Render in browser with JavaScript. Use 'use client' directive. Fetch data in useEffect or on mount. Good for interactive components, dashboards. App Router uses Client Components for interactivity, Server Components for data fetching.
client_side_rendering.example: 'use client'; export default function Component() { useEffect(() => { fetchData(); }, []); }

css_modules.statement: CSS Modules: Create .module.css files. Import: import styles from './Component.module.css'. Use: <div className={styles.container}>. Scoped CSS (class names hashed). Supported in both App Router and Pages Router.

global_css.statement: Global CSS: Import in app/layout.js or _app.js: import './globals.css'. Global styles apply to entire app. Only one global CSS file. Use for reset styles, fonts, CSS variables. App Router: import in root layout only.

tailwind_css.statement: Tailwind CSS: Install with create-next-app or manually. Configure tailwind.config.js. Import in globals.css: @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;. Use utility classes in JSX. Fully supported in Next.js.

image_optimization.statement: next/image component automatically optimizes images: import Image from 'next/image'; <Image src="/photo.jpg" width={500} height={300} alt="Photo" />. Features: lazy loading, responsive images, WebP/AVIF format conversion, blur placeholder. Requires width and height (or fill for responsive).

font_optimization.statement: next/font optimizes fonts: import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'; const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'] });. Use in layout: <body className={inter.className}>. Automatic font optimization, self-hosted fonts, zero layout shift. Supports Google Fonts and custom fonts.
font_optimization.example: import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'; const inter = Inter(); <body className={inter.className}>

script_optimization.statement: next/script optimizes third-party scripts: import Script from 'next/script'; <Script src="https://example.com/script.js" strategy="afterInteractive" />. Strategies: 'beforeInteractive' (before page interactive), 'afterInteractive' (after page interactive, default), 'lazyOnload' (on window load).

legacy_routing.statement: Pages Router (legacy): File-system routing in pages/ directory. pages/index.js → /, pages/about.js → /about. Use getServerSideProps for SSR, getStaticProps for SSG, getStaticPaths for dynamic SSG. API routes in pages/api/. Still supported but App Router recommended for new projects.
legacy_routing.example:
pages/about.js: export default function About() { return
About
; }

getserversideprops.statement: getServerSideProps runs on server on each request (SSR). Export: export async function getServerSideProps(context) { return { props: { data } }; }. Context includes params, query, req, res. Run server-side code, fetch data, return props. Deprecated in App Router (use Server Components instead).
getserversideprops.example: export async function getServerSideProps() { const data = await fetchData(); return { props: { data } }; }

getstaticprops.statement: getStaticProps runs at build time (SSG). Export: export async function getStaticProps(context) { return { props: { data }, revalidate: 60 }; }. Revalidate for ISR. Pre-renders page at build. Deprecated in App Router (use Server Components with fetch caching).
getstaticprops.example: export async function getStaticProps() { const data = await fetchData(); return { props: { data }, revalidate: 3600 }; }

vercel.statement: Vercel is recommended deployment platform (created by Next.js team). Zero-configuration deployment. Automatic HTTPS, global CDN, preview deployments for PRs, environment variables management. Connect GitHub repository, auto-deploy on push. Free tier available.
vercel.example: Deploy to Vercel: vercel deploy or connect GitHub repo in Vercel dashboard

docker.statement: Docker deployment: Use Next.js standalone output. next build outputs .next/standalone directory. Create Dockerfile with Node.js, copy standalone files, run next start. Supports standalone server mode for containerized deployments.

static_export.statement: Static Export: Configure output: 'export' in next.config.js. Run next build to generate static HTML. Export: export default { output: 'export' }. Pages must not use server-side features (getServerSideProps, API routes, Server Components with dynamic data).
static_export.example: next.config.js: module.exports = { output: 'export' }; // Generate static HTML

typescript_support.statement: Next.js has excellent TypeScript support. Install types: npm install -D typescript @types/react @types/node. Create tsconfig.json (auto-generated by create-next-app). File extensions: .ts, .tsx. Type-safe routing, API routes, Server Actions. Full TypeScript support in both App Router and Pages Router.
typescript_support.example: app/page.tsx, app/api/users/route.ts, app/layout.tsx all support TypeScript

basic_app_router_page.title: Basic App Router Page

server_component_data_fetching.title: Server Component Data Fetching

server_action.title: Server Action

route_handler.title: Route Handler (API Route)

notes.maintenance: Update when Next.js releases new versions or adds new features. Check nextjs.org/docs for latest documentation and migration guides
notes.coverage: Covers Next.js 16 with focus on App Router. Pages Router documented for legacy support. Update for new App Router features and rendering strategies.
